ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken soup' class (FOODON_00002809); no specific 'chicken tortilla soup' class exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated chicken tortilla soup entry. 'Chicken soup' (A0CKP) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for chicken tortilla soup. General food concepts in SNOMED CT do not extend to this level of specificity.

allergens: dairy (if sour cream or cheese garnish included)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant bowl of chicken tortilla soup, approximately 350 g, including broth, shredded chicken, vegetables, and tortilla strips.

nutrient_source.title: Soup, chicken tortilla, restaurant-prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central data for restaurant-style chicken tortilla soup (NDB No. 06972 and related composite entries). Values represent a typical restaurant-prepared version with chicken, tomato-chili broth, corn tortilla strips, black beans, and corn. Per-100g values are calculated from the composite. Minor micronutrients not reported by USDA are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for mixed chicken and vegetable soup with tortilla
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken tortilla soup in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed vegetable and chicken soups with corn-based components. Corn tortilla strips have a GI of approximately 52 (University of Sydney GI Database); the dilution effect of the broth, protein, fibre from beans, and fat lowers the overall dish GI to an estimated 45. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (45 × 20.3 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 9.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from broth and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Chicken tortilla soup contains approximately 310 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1085 mg per standard 350 g bowl), which represents nearly half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should request low-sodium broth, limit portion size, or avoid added salt and high-sodium garnishes such as cheese.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from tortilla strips, corn, and beans with low-to-moderate GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 45 and a glycemic load of approximately 9 per standard serving, chicken tortilla soup has a moderate glycaemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, limit fried tortilla strip quantity, and pair the soup with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from chicken and optional dairy garnishes
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content and moderate saturated fat (approximately 0.5 g per 100 g, rising with cheese or sour cream garnishes) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose skinless chicken, request low-sodium broth, and avoid high-fat garnishes to keep saturated fat and sodium within heart-healthy limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tortilla Soup is a classic Mexican soup dish consisting of a rich, spiced tomato-chili broth simmered with shredded chicken, black beans, corn kernels, onion, garlic, jalapeño, cumin, and chili powder. The soup is traditionally finished with crispy fried or baked corn tortilla strips that soften slightly in the hot broth, adding a characteristic corn flavour and textural contrast. Common garnishes include shredded cheese, sour cream, diced avocado, fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice. The dish is savoury, mildly to moderately spicy, and deeply flavourful with a smoky, tangy profile from the tomatoes and chili. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g restaurant bowl provides approximately 182 kcal, 14.4 g protein, 6.3 g fat, and 20.3 g carbohydrate. The soup is a moderate source of dietary fibre from beans and corn, and provides meaningful amounts of potassium, vitamin A, vitamin C, and B vitamins from the vegetable and chicken components. Sodium content is high due to the seasoned broth, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycemic index of 45 places it in the low GI category, making it a relatively suitable option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when tortilla strip quantity is moderated. In Singapore, this dish is served at Mexican restaurants such as Three Amigos and Fiesta Mexico, typically priced at SGD 15 per bowl.

confidence_reason: No exact match for chicken tortilla soup was found in a primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central composite data for restaurant-style chicken tortilla soup, which is the closest approved source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on comparable mixed soups with corn-based components. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central (NDB 06972 and related composite entries) for restaurant-prepared chicken tortilla soup. Values represent a typical preparation; actual nutrient content will vary by restaurant recipe, garnish choices, and portion size. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite for this dish. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite for this dish. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite for this dish. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed as negligible/trace in the USDA source for this dish type. 6. GI is an analogous estimate of 45 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for comparable mixed chicken and vegetable soups with corn-based components; no direct GI measurement for chicken tortilla soup was identified in the approved GI database. 7. The dish is served at Two Singapore restaurants on field survey: Fiesta Mexico - Three Amigos Indian and Mexican Restaurant, and Three Amigos Mexican and Indian - Boat Quay, at SGD 15.00. 8. Halal status is marked true as chicken-based soups without pork or alcohol are typically halal-certifiable; however, individual restaurant certification should be verified. 9. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy the Singapore restaurant context multilingual requirement, as this dish is served in Singapore. 10. FoodOn match type is closest_parent (FOODON_00002809, chicken soup) as no specific chicken tortilla soup class exists in FoodOn. FoodEx2 match type is closest_parent (A0CKP, chicken soup) for the same reason.

improvement_actions_taken: Populated all multilingual local names (Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to satisfy Singapore restaurant context multilingual requirement.
Derived per_100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central composite for restaurant-style chicken tortilla soup.
Estimated GI at 45 (low) using University of Sydney GI Database analogous data for mixed chicken and vegetable soups with corn-based components; documented estimation basis in gi_source.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving as (45 × 20.3 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 9.1.
Set vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, and selenium_mcg to -1 with explicit documentation in notes as required by G3 sentinel values rule.
Confirmed trans_fat_g as 0.0 (trace/negligible per USDA source) and documented in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Used only allowed match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none) for all ontology fields.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key 'type2_diabetes'.
Set confidence to medium with justification referencing adapted USDA source and analogous GI estimate.
